name: Paint

tools.Pencil: UIA name 'Pencil' â€” freehand drawing tool
tools.Fill with color: UIA name 'Fill with color' â€” flood fill tool
tools.Color 1: UIA name 'Color 1' â€” click before Edit Colors to set primary color
tools.Edit colors: UIA name 'Edit colors' â€” opens RGB color picker dialog
tools.Text: UIA name 'Text' â€” click then click canvas to place text box

known_issues: Canvas size != window size. The canvas is a specific region inside the window. ALWAYS use get_canvas_bounds() which reads the actual canvas dimensions from Paint's status bar (e.g. '795 Ã— 605px'). NEVER assume canvas fills the window.
Canvas resize handles at edges â€” stay 15px away from all canvas borders. If a drag starts/ends too close to the edge, it resizes the canvas instead of drawing.
Edit Colors dialog edits Color 2 (secondary) if Color 1 is not clicked first. Always click 'Color 1' UIA element before opening Edit Colors.
Ctrl+W opens Resize and Skew dialog, NOT close. Use kill_app() or Alt+F4 to close Paint.
Brush size selection via map_screen is dangerous â€” 'size' partial-matches 'Resize' button and opens Resize and Skew dialog. Don't attempt brush size changes.
If Paint is NOT maximized, toolbar coordinates differ. Always maximize before drawing.
Ctrl+S saves in-place if file was previously saved. Use F12 for Save As to specify a new path.
Save As dialog: filename field is pre-focused. Pasting replaces selected text directly. Do NOT use Ctrl+A in Save As â€” it selects files in the list, not text.
After Ctrl+N (new canvas), a 'Save changes?' dialog may appear. Press 'N' for Don't Save.
tips: ALWAYS use get_canvas_bounds() to detect canvas area â€” it reads from Paint's status bar via UIA. Returns (left, top, right, bottom) in screen coords.
ALWAYS maximize Paint before drawing for consistent coordinates: ensure_maximized('Paint')
Use select_color('red') for colors â€” it opens Edit Colors dialog and sets exact RGB via pywinauto auto_ids 706/707/708. More reliable than clicking palette swatches.
Use app_save(filepath, 'Paint') or paint_save(filepath) to save â€” handles focus, Save As dialog, format confirmation, and verification automatically.
Use new_canvas() to reset to blank instead of kill+reopen â€” faster and handles Save dialog.
For drawing: pick color FIRST with select_color(), THEN draw. Never draw then repaint.

NEVER use flood fill (use_fill) on pencil-drawn shapes — pencil lines have pixel gaps so fill leaks and destroys the entire canvas. Only use fill ONCE at the very start to set a background color on a blank canvas, then switch to pencil and NEVER switch back to fill. Draw everything with outlines only.
Canvas top-left is approximately (5, 144) when maximized on 1920x1080, but ALWAYS verify with get_canvas_bounds().
Plan the composition before drawing. Draw each element exactly once. Simple and clean beats complex and messy.
